Internode

Your Wiki Is Going Stale Because of How You Write, Not Who Writes.

Pages are an output of writing. Decisions are an output of working. When those are two separate workflows, the wiki always loses. The fix is not more discipline. The fix is a knowledge model where work is the input and structured records are the output.
It is a workflow problem, not a people problem.

Try AI-first knowledge management

3 months free. No credit card.

The full picture

How an AI-first knowledge model breaks the decay chain in one image.

The decay chain

  • 🎙️

    The truth lives in conversations

    Decisions, tradeoffs, owners, and reasoning all happen in real time. On Zoom, in standups, on customer calls. Nobody opens Confluence after the meeting to write down why.

  • 📋

    Writing is a separate workflow

    The wiki page only exists when someone makes time to write it. Most people do not. The pages that do exist become snapshots of what someone thought once. Two weeks later, half of it is wrong.

  • 🌀

    Slack becomes the real wiki

    When the page is missing or stale, the team asks in Slack. The answer disappears into a thread. New hires drown. The wiki you maintain stops being the wiki you use.

The four moves

  1. 1

    Make conversations the input

    Record Zoom, Meet, calls, or ad hoc discussions. Internode plugs into the tools your team already uses.

  2. 2

    Extract structured outcomes

    Decisions, tasks, topics, goals, owners, even rejected options. The outcome lives somewhere other than a transcript.

  3. 3

    Link every record to its source

    Each decision points back to the meeting, person, task, and prior decision. The trail is always there.

  4. 4

    Propose updates automatically

    When reality drifts, the system flags the change. Humans approve instead of rewriting pages.

Yeah but...

Is this just an AI meeting bot?

Meeting bots give you a transcript and a summary. Internode gives you a connected decision graph: decisions, owners, tasks, and prior reasoning, linked to every source moment. The transcript is a byproduct, not the output. More on the difference: AI meeting notes vs organizational memory.

What happens to our existing wiki?

Internode does not replace your wiki. It changes the source of truth. The working layer (conversations, calls, chats) becomes the input, structured records become the source, and Internode proposes updates to your wiki when reality drifts. Humans still approve. See Internode vs Notion as a wiki and an AI-native alternative to Notion.

Will my team actually use it?

That is the point: there is nothing for them to use. Internode plugs into the meetings, calls, and tools your team already uses. The behavior change is 'do nothing.' Knowledge accumulates as a side effect of working. Read more on the knowledge system that builds itself.

Why is the old wiki model broken?

Pages are an output of writing. Decisions are an output of working. When you ask one workflow (working) to feed a different workflow (writing), the wiki loses every time the team is busy. AI-first means those workflows are the same workflow. Background reading: what institutional knowledge is and why teams lose it and an AI knowledge base that builds itself.

What is the catch with three months free?

No catch. No credit card. We are confident: once your team has connected, structured knowledge that updates itself, going back to a snapshot wiki feels like a downgrade you cannot unsee.

Run the 30-minute test.

Pick your next real product call. Record it. Run it through Internode. Compare the output to your hand notes, or to your stale wiki page. If the output is better than the page nobody wrote, the model works.

Start the 30-minute test

3 months free. No credit card.
